text:
WESTERN EUROPE

3. French seek support of German Socialist Party for plan to neutralize Germany:

Since the middle of August, French occupation officials in Germany have reportedly been circulating a document outlining a proposed plan for a future French policy in Germany.

This proposal, which is allegedly supported by West German Socialist leader Erich Ollenhauer, includes: (1) a French veto of any German army linked to NATO; (2) opposition to any American effort to raise a defense force in West Germany; and (3) a four-power agreement on all-German elections for a neutralized, lightly armed Germany. The elections would be supervised by UN members other than the United States and the Soviet Union and would be administered by an "electoral government" drawn from the West German and Soviet zone parliaments
